rationale: The citing paper’s core mechanism is a reconstructive-memory spillover from employees’ digital presence, while the cited paper develops an Internet-cue/transactive-memory mechanism (delegating memory to online availability, increasing confidence in information access) that is conceptually close. Because both study how online-information cues shape offline evaluations/choices, this citation could plausibly be a theory/mechanism source rather than a generic mention, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
